objective:
FICHE_STRATEGIE v1
id: trend_supertrend
archetype: trend_supertrend
family: trend
timeframe: 1h
side: both
indicators:
- supertrend(atr_period=5, multiplier=2.0)
- adx(period=23)
- atr(period=14)
entry:
- long: supertrend.direction == 1 and adx.adx > 30
- short: supertrend.direction == -1 and adx.adx > 30
exit:
- condition: direction_change(supertrend.direction) or adx.adx < 20
risk:
stop_atr_mult: 2.25
tp_atr_mult: 4.5
description: ATR trailing stop (2.25x) and take-profit (4.5x)
constraints:
- no_lookahead: true
- only_registry_indicators: true

iterations.hypothesis: A combination of Supertrend, ADX and ATR indicators with proper logic can provide a robust trading strategy in trending markets.

iterations.hypothesis: ATR trailing stop (2.25x) and take-profit (4.5x) with trend filter based on ATR, RSI and ADX.
Using supertrend trend direction along with adx for momentum and atr as a filter to identify trending markets.

code.validation_error_retry: [SIG001] `for i in range(...)` interdit dans la logique Builder.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] The strategy uses supertrend to identify trend direction, adx for momentum and atr as a filter. It sets stop loss at 2.25 times ATR and take profit at 4.5 times ATR with no lookahead or only registry indicators used. Hypothesis is based on using these indicators together to generate profitable trades in trending markets but results are yet unknown.
[Backup plan] 1) Weaknesses could be false signals due to noise in the data, so backtesting should include more historical data for robustness 2) If returns are poor, adjusting stop loss and take profit parameters might improve performance. Adx threshold may also need tweaking depending on market conditions. 3) Adjusting ATR multiplier or using different ATR periods could enhance Sharpe ratio without compromising trend detection ability. Alternative approach would be to use more complex machine learning models if this one doesn't perform well.
